title: Ecosystem command surface (one file per ecosystem)
description: Schema for a consuming repository's .claude/ecosystems/<ecosystem>.yaml file. The filename stem is the ecosystem identifier (lowercase kebab-case). Command values are opaque shell strings; null means the ecosystem has no such phase. Contract: docs/conventions/ecosystem-commands/README.md.

enabled.description: Whether this ecosystem applies to the repo. False disables detection without deleting the file.

globs.description: File glob patterns that classify changed files into this ecosystem (matched against repo-relative paths).

anchor.description: How to resolve <solution-or-project-file>: the scoping anchor for ecosystems with a canonical entry point (e.g. 'the solution file at repo root (*.slnx)').

project-discovery.description: File patterns identifying per-project roots (walked for <project-dir>), e.g. ['pyproject.toml'].

build-cmd.description: Build/compile verification command. Null when the ecosystem has no build step.

test-cmd.description: Test command. Null when no test framework is wired.

check-cmd.description: Lint/format check command (no file modification). Null when lint does not apply.

fix-cmd.description: Auto-fix command. Null when the toolchain has no fix mode.

opt-in.description: The config file or condition whose presence signals the repo uses this toolchain (e.g. 'biome.json'). Informational for resolvers deciding applicability.

install-hint.description: Cross-platform install guidance shown when a required tool is missing. Missing tools report skip, never failure.

gates.description: Repo-specific CI-parity gates beyond plain build/test/lint (lockfile drift, generated-artifact freshness, schema regeneration).

trigger-globs.description: Run the gate only when a changed file matches; omit to run whenever the ecosystem runs.

notes.description: Free-form caveats a resolver should surface (trap flags, ordering constraints). Rationale belongs in the repo's own rules, not here.
